title: applehv: Rosetta support
body:
This PR adds Rosetta support to the AppleHV Podman machine(only v5).
Rosetta is only available on macOS with Apple Silicon.
With Rosetta, the execution performance is several times better than QEMU emulation.
https://developer.apple.com/documentation/virtualization/running_intel_binaries_in_linux_vms_with_rosetta

Todo
- [x] GOARCH and Provider check
- [x] ~~CLI option docs~~
- [x] Appropriate location in machine configuration(or containers common)
- [x] `rosetta.InstallRosetta` vfkit option
- [x] containers/common PR https://github.com/containers/common/pull/1877
- [x] Release notes
- [x] Consideration of SELinux context
- [x] ship the unit file/script in the Containerfile https://github.com/containers/podman-machine-os/pull/7
- [x] Checking Test Code Execution


> [!NOTE] 
> [SELinux context] > resolved
> ~~Perhaps because of the use of vfkit, the SELinux context needs to be with `*exec_t` when mounting the Rosetta directory.~~
> ~~On lima and UTM which support Rosetta, it can be executed with `nfs_t` without error.~~
> 
> ~~https://github.com/lima-vm/lima/blob/88c89165273d87b33753a593af867b20c1d1c67d/pkg/cidata/cidata.TEMPLATE.d/boot/05-rosetta-volume.sh#L22~~

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```release-note
Podman machine can now use Rosetta 2 (a.k.a Rosetta) on macOS with Apple Silicon. This is enabled by default. If you wish to change this option, you can set via containers.conf.
```

[no new tests needed]

comments.body: Why would a user ever disable this?  Shouldn't this be on by default?

To activate Rosetta, you must mount the Rosetta directory on your Linux OS and add the magic code to binfmt_misc. Some users may want to remove this as an afterthought.
The default can be turned on (true).
Ephemeral COPR build failed. @containers/packit-build please check.

Man page needs to be updated as well.

@edsantiago are we verifying man pages for podman machine?

Yes, `podman machine` is checked. CI just didn't get to that check because of other earlier failures. If CI had made it past those, it would've failed with:
```
xref-helpmsgs-manpages: 'podman machine init --help' lists '--rosetta', which is not in docs/source/markdown/podman-machine-init.1.md
xref-helpmsgs-manpages: 'podman machine inspect --format <TAB>' lists '.Rosetta', which is not in docs/source/markdown/podman-machine-inspect.1.md
xref-helpmsgs-manpages: 'podman machine set --help' lists '--rosetta', which is not in docs/source/markdown/podman-machine-set.1.md
```
Hi, before you push again, *please rebase on current main*. This will be necessary to check man pages and to avoid breaking future main. Thank you!

I agree that containers.conf in the machine section is the proper place for this. I don't think the CLI option is necessary since I don't think people will be changing back and forth. I do believe most users will want Rosetta=true, since building x86 images on Mac Arm platforms is going to be very common.
Thanks for the contribution! This looks super promising! One small formatting nit: all files need to end with a newline.

Thank you all for your comments and reviews.
The implementation of common (containers.conf) and the removal of the `--rosetta` option(init/set) is working well in my environment.

My understanding is that I need to send a PR to containers/common ,right?

github.com/containers/common/pkg/config/default.go
```
Rosetta: true,
```

github.com/containers/common/pkg/config/config.go
```
// Rosetta
Rosetta bool `toml: "rosetta,omitempty"`
```

Sorry for the late comment:

>@rhatdan:I do believe most users will want Rosetta=true, since building x86 images on Mac Arm platforms is going to be very common.

I agree this should just always be on for apple silicon. The only reason to change this is in the rare case you want qemu-user-static to take precedence, which would be a fairly specialized situation. Only case I think of that happening is if there is some bug in rosetta, which breaks a container you need to run, so you need to temporarily apply a workaround. 

> @rhatdan:   I agree that containers.conf in the machine section is the proper place for this. I don't think the CLI option is necessary since I don't think people will be changing back and forth.  

IMO there are two issues with it being only specifiable in containers.conf:

1. The machine section is a global, yet the setting scope is really per-machine. Adding it could be reasonable as a default, but if its something that is rare then it might not be necessary
2. Introduces UX complexity, since to set it you first need to write a containers.conf file, and then run the CLI. If you need to change it you then need to set it, delete the machine and then recreate it. It also introduces a similar UX contract API interaction complexity with Podman Desktop, since it needs to incorporate containers.conf editing when this particular change is made.

I think it's inevitable that we will need provider specific settings, but agree it can create a problem with MxN parameter clutter across providers.  I can think of two ways we could handle that

1. We could use a generic option for provider settings using name/value arrays  (e.g. --provider-setting-name="foo" --provider-setting-value="blah" --provider-setting-name="other" --provider-setting-value="thing"). These would be passed straight into the provider as a map 
2. We could have a phase were each provider gets a hook to register extra flags under an opaque type  as part of the cobra init, and then perhaps we can add a common prefix convention to make the docs cleaner.  Some made up hypothetical examples `--applehv-rosetta=false`, `--applehv-disk-cache=automatic` `--hyperv-dynamic-memory=true` `--wsl-wayland=true`

Although not suggesting we do this now. With Rosetta could just wait until someone actually needs it to be turned off.

I wrote a simple process flow (each labels and vars are not an exact name, but to get a sense of the true/false flow).
(mermaid graphs are not displayed on GitHub mobile app)

> [!WARNING]
> Outdated at 4/5
## init
### AppleHV(Arm) Default: true
```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant U as User
    participant F as Init() @host.go
    participant MC as machine config *.json
    participant M as mc.AppleHypervisor.Vfkit.Rosetta
    participant S as createOpts.Rosetta
    participant D as defaultMachineConfig() @default.go

    U->>F: machine init
    F->>D: registry.PodmanConfig()
    D->>S: rosetta=true
    S->>M: mc.AppleHypervisor.Vfkit.Rosetta = opts.Rosetta
    M->>MC: Rosetta: true
```

### AppleHV(Arm) set to rosetta=false
```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant U as User
    participant F as Init() @host.go
    participant MC as machine config *.json
    participant M as mc.AppleHypervisor.Vfkit.Rosetta
    participant S as createOpts.Rosetta
	participant E as ENV
    participant C as containers.conf

    U->>F: machine init
    F->>C: registry.PodmanConfig()
	alt containers.conf
    	C->>S: rosetta=false (or rosetta=true)
        loop
            S->>S: createOpts.Rosetta = false
        end
    else CONTAINERS_MACHINE_ROSETTA
        E->>S: CONTAINERS_MACHINE_ROSETTA=false
        loop
            S->>S: createOpts.Rosetta = false
        end
    end
    S->>M: mc.AppleHypervisor.Vfkit.Rosetta = opts.Rosetta
    M->>MC: Rosetta: false
```

### Other Provider + AppleHV(Intel)
```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant U as User
    participant F as Init() @host.go
    participant MC as machine config *.json
    participant M as mc.AppleHypervisor.Vfkit.Rosetta
    participant S as createOpts.Rosetta
    participant D as defaultMachineConfig() @default.go

    U->>F: machine init
    F->>D: registry.PodmanConfig()
    D->>S: rosetta=true
    loop check VMtype,GOARCH
        S->>S:createOpts.Rosetta = false
    end
    S->>M: mc.AppleHypervisor.Vfkit.Rosetta = opts.Rosetta
    M->>MC: Rosetta: false
```
## start
### AppleHV(Arm) Rosetta: true
```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant U as User
    participant F as Start() @host.go
    participant R as rosetta
    participant RN as rosettaNew
    participant M as mc.AppleHypervisor.Vfkit.Rosetta
    participant MC as machine config *.json

    U->>F: machine start
    F->>MC: mc *vmconfigs.MachineConfig
    MC->>M: Rosetta: true
    M->>RN: rosettNew = mc.AppleHypervisor.Vfkit.Rosetta
    RN->>R: rosetta = rosettaNew
```

### AppleHV(Arm) Rosetta: true to false
```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant U as User
    participant F as Start() @host.go
    participant C as container.conf
    participant E as ENV
    participant RN as rosettaNew
    participant R as rosetta
    participant M as mc.AppleHypervisor.Vfkit.Rosetta
    participant MC as machine config *.json

    U->>F: machine start
    F->>MC: mc *vmconfigs.MachineConfig
    MC->>M: Rosetta: true
    M->>RN: rosettaNew = mc.AppleHypervisor.Vfkit.Rosetta
    alt containers.conf
        C->>F: rosetta=false
        loop
            F->>F: rosettaCfg = false
        end
        F->>RN: rosettaNew = rosettaCfg
    else CONTAINERS_MACHINE_ROSETTA
        E->>F: CONTAINERS_MACHINE_ROSETTA=false
        loop
            F->>F: rosettaOverride = false
        end
        F->>RN: rosettaNew = rosettaOverride

    end
    RN->>R: rosetta = rosettaNew
    R->>M: mc.AppleHypervisor.Vfkit.Rosetta = rosetta
    M->>MC: Rosetta: false
```
### Other Provider + AppleHV(Intel)
```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant U as User
    participant F as Start() @host.go
    participant S as SetRosetta() @stubber.go
    participant C as container.conf
    participant E as ENV

    participant MC as machine config *.json

    U->>F: machine start
    F->>S: SetRosetta(mc)
    S->>F: return nil
```

Here is my plan for Rosetta activation.

Activating Rosetta requires two steps.
1. Mount the Rosetta directory
2. Register the Rosetta runtime binary as the handler

You can use systemd-binfmt to handle step 2, but first you need to complete step 1.

There are ways to run the unit file that handles step 1 before systemd-binfmt.
For example `Before=systemd-binfmt` is one way to do this.
However, this will result in an error because the dependency cannot be resolved.

```
Mar 03 02:58:59 localhost.localdomain systemd[1]: /etc/systemd/system/rosetta-mount.service:3: Failed to add dependency on systemd-binfmt, ignoring: Invalid argument
```

This is the Rosetta activation flow I implemented in this PR.
After completion of systemd-binfmt, the process must be branched by determining the Rosetta flag.
Therefore, rosetta-activation.service is disabled to avoid automatic execution.

> [!WARNING]
> Outdated at 3/6
```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A(podman machine start)
    B[FCOS boot]
    C[systemd-binfmt.service]
    D[rosetta-activation.service]
    E[func ActivateRosettaService]
    F[mount the rosetta directory]
    G[unregister the qemu-x86_64 handler]
    H[register the rosetta handler]
    Z(End)

    A -->B
    B -->|systemd| C
    C -->|...after API socket started| D
    D{Rosetta flag}
    D-->|true| E
    E-->|podman machine ssh\n sudo systemctl start\n rosetta-activation.service| rosetta-activation.service
        subgraph rosetta-activation.service
            F --> G
            G --> H
        end
    rosetta-activation.service --> Z
    D -->|false| Z
   ```

> There are ways to run the unit file that handles step 1 before systemd-binfmt.
For example Before=systemd-binfmt is one way to do this.
However, this will result in an error because the dependency cannot be resolved.

With a simple unit file, this works for me without a dependency issue:
```
$ cat /etc/systemd/system/test.service
[Unit]
Description=example systemd service unit file.
Before=systemd-binfmt.service

[Service]
ExecStart=/bin/bash /usr/local/bin/example.sh

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target

$ cat /usr/local/bin/example.sh 
#!/bin/sh

echo "hello world" >/usr/local/share/helloworld.txt

$ sudo systemctl daemon-reload
$ sudo systemctl enable test.service
$ sudo systemctl reboot
```

Enabled rosetta-activation.service and removed the process of executing services via ssh.
The systemd error when `rosetta=false` has been fixed by ensuring that ExecStart* always ends with true.
`/bin/sh -c "~ || true"`

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A(podman machine start)
    B[getDefaultDevices]
    C{Rosetta flag}
    D[append rosetta]
    E[FCOS boot]
    F[systemd-binfmt.service]
    G[mount the rosetta directory]
    H[register the rosetta handler]
    I{/mnt/rosetta}
    J[unregister the qemu-x86_64 handler]
    Z(End)

    A -->B
    B -->C
    C -->|true| D
    C -->|false| E
    D -->E
    E -->|systemd| F
    F -->|After=systemd-binfmt.service| rosetta-activation.service
        subgraph rosetta-activation.service
            G -->|If Rosetta flag is false, then error. \n But return is true. | H
        end
    rosetta-activation.service --> I
        subgraph unregister-handler.service
            I -->|true| J
        end
    J -->Z
    I -->|false|Z
   ```

> With a simple unit file, this works for me without a dependency issue:

I have one common issue with Rosetta in LIMA is, it gets replaced with qemu if qemu is updated on Fedora OS. Not sure how it would be on core os but on regular os it happens. I just restart the machine to get it fixed again after qemu updates. Just dropping this info here incase if it helps.

> I have one common issue with Rosetta in LIMA is, it gets replaced with qemu if qemu is updated on Fedora OS. Not sure how it would be on core os but on regular os it happens. I just restart the machine to get it fixed again after qemu updates. Just dropping this info here incase if it helps.

This can be avoided with `ln -s  /dev/null /etc/binfmt.d/qemu-x86_64-static.conf`
From [`man binfmt.d`](https://www.man7.org/linux/man-pages/man5/binfmt.d.5.html):

> If the administrator wants to disable a configuration file supplied by the vendor, the recommended way is to place a symlink to /dev/null in the configuration directory in /etc/, with the same filename as the vendor configuration file.

I am starting to customize machine-os, but I have a problem.
The systemd services I added are not enabled.
Is there any effective way to get around this?

machine-os's Containerfile
```
# For Rosetta
COPY rosetta-activation.service /etc/systemd/system/rosetta-activation.service
COPY unregister-handler.service /etc/systemd/system/unregister-handler.service
COPY rosetta-activation.sh /usr/local/bin/rosetta-activation.sh
RUN systemctl enable rosetta-activation.service unregister-handler.service && \
    ostree container commit
```

test podman machine
```
root@localhost:/var/home/core# systemctl status rosetta-activation.service 
○ rosetta-activation.service - Activates Rosetta if necessary
     Loaded: loaded (/etc/systemd/system/rosetta-activation.service; disabled; preset: disabled)
    Drop-In: /usr/lib/systemd/system/service.d
             └─10-timeout-abort.conf
     Active: inactive (dead)
root@localhost:/var/home/core# systemctl status unregister-handler.service
○ unregister-handler.service - Unregiester x86_64 handler if necessary
     Loaded: loaded (/etc/systemd/system/unregister-handler.service; disabled; preset: disabled)

             └─10-timeout-abort.conf
     Active: inactive (dead)
 ```

Update the Rosetta true/false flows.
(mermaid graphs are not displayed on GitHub mobile app)

## init
### AppleHV(Arm) Default: true
```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant U as User
    participant F as initMachine() @init.go
    participant MC as machine config *.json
    participant M as mc.AppleHypervisor.Vfkit.Rosetta
    participant S as createOpts.Rosetta
    participant D as defaultMachineConfig() @default.go

    U->>F: machine init
    F->>D: registry.PodmanConfig()
    D->>S: rosetta=true
    S->>M: mc.AppleHypervisor.Vfkit.Rosetta = opts.Rosetta
    M->>MC: Rosetta: true
```

### AppleHV(Arm) set containers.conf to rosetta=false
```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant U as User
    participant F as initMachine() @init.go
    participant MC as machine config *.json
    participant M as mc.AppleHypervisor.Vfkit.Rosetta
    participant S as createOpts.Rosetta
    participant C as containers.conf

    U->>F: machine init
    F->>C: registry.PodmanConfig()
    C->>S: rosetta=false
    S->>M: mc.AppleHypervisor.Vfkit.Rosetta = opts.Rosetta
    M->>MC: Rosetta: false
```

### Other Provider + AppleHV(Intel)
```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant U as User
    participant F as initMachine() @init.go
    participant MC as machine config *.json
    participant M as mc.AppleHypervisor.Vfkit.Rosetta
    participant S as createOpts.Rosetta
    participant D as defaultMachineConfig() @default.go

    U->>F: machine init
    F->>D: registry.PodmanConfig()
    D->>S: rosetta=true
    loop check VMtype,GOARCH
        S->>S:createOpts.Rosetta = false
    end
    S->>M: mc.AppleHypervisor.Vfkit.Rosetta = opts.Rosetta
    M->>MC: Rosetta: false
```

## start
### AppleHV(Arm) Rosetta: true
```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant U as User
    participant F as start() @start.go
    participant S as startOpts.Rosetta
    participant D as defaultMachineConfig() @default.go

    U->>F: machine start
    F->>D: registry.PodmanConfig()
    D->>S: rosetta=true
```

### AppleHV(Arm) Rosetta: true→false
```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant U as User
    participant F as Start() @host.go

    participant MC as machine config *.json
    participant M as mc.AppleHypervisor.Vfkit.Rosetta

    participant RN as rosettaNew
    participant S as startOpts.Rosetta
    participant C as container.conf

    U->>F: machine start
    F->>C: registry.PodmanConfig()
    C->>S: rosetta=false

    MC->>M: Rosetta: true
    M->>RN: rosettaNew = mc.AppleHypervisor.Vfkit.Rosetta
    S->>RN: rosettaNew = rosetta

    RN->>M: mc.AppleHypervisor.Vfkit.Rosetta = rosettaNew
    M->>MC: Rosetta: false
```

### Other Provider + AppleHV(Intel)
```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant U as User
    participant F as Start() @host.go
    participant SR as SetRosetta() @stubber.go
    participant S as startOpts.Rosetta
    participant D as defaultMachineConfig() @default.go

    U->>F: machine start
    F->>D: registry.PodmanConfig()
    D->>S: rosetta=true
    F->>SR: SetRosetta(mc)
    SR->>F: return nil
```

What happens with this change if you try to run a container image that exceeds the limits of the Rosetta software implementation? For example:

```
podman run --rm --arch amd64 -it quay.io/fedoraci/fedora:eln
```

Or perhaps this (although CentOS 10 CPU compatibility diagnostics are currently busted, sorry):

```
podman run --rm --arch amd64 -it centos:stream10-development
```

We worked with QEMU upstream (for TCG) and valgrind to make sure they are compatible with these upcoming distribution changes, but we couldn't do the same for Rosetta.

@fweimer-rh 
> ```
> podman run --rm --arch amd64 -it quay.io/fedoraci/fedora:eln
>Fatal glibc error: CPU does not support x86-64-v3
> ```
> ```
> podman run --rm --arch amd64 -it centos:stream10-development
>(response will be stuck)
> ```
When using rosetta, it is not possible to run both container images.
This is a specification of Apple's Rosetta and is beyond our control.
https://developer.apple.com/documentation/apple-silicon/about-the-rosetta-translation-environment
@tnk4on Understood. I was wondering if these images stop working in the default configuration once this change is merged. How is the way execution selected?

> We worked with QEMU upstream (for TCG) and valgrind to make sure they are compatible with these upcoming distribution changes, but we couldn't do the same for Rosetta.

I've reported this rosetta limitation through https://feedbackassistant.apple.com/, we'll see if this eventually results in a fix :)
